field_types.target_item_id: string (item_id of the proposal being reviewed)
field_types.target_commander: string (commander who authored the reviewed proposal)
field_types.verdict: string (observed: accept_with_additions, approve_with_suggestions, approve, endorse, endorse_with_caveat)

validation_gaps: verdict field not in protocol required_fields but used by all 11 observed reviews
target_item_id and target_commander not in required_fields but essential for linkage
No enum constraint on verdict values - each commander uses different vocabulary
evidence field sometimes absent in reviews despite being in required_fields

field_types.evidence: array of strings (file:line refs) or string (inconsistent)
field_types.based_on: array of strings (item_ids of source proposals/reviews)
field_types.source_item_id: string (alternative to based_on, used by commander-003)
field_types.source_refs: array of strings (file paths with optional line numbers)

validation_gaps: based_on vs source_item_id inconsistency across commanders
evidence type inconsistent: array vs string across commanders
No schema enforcer validates events before append
based_on is not in required_fields but is the primary linkage mechanism

findings.linkage: Reviews link to proposals via target_item_id (referencing proposal item_id). Improvements link to proposals and reviews via based_on array (containing item_ids from proposals/reviews). These linkage fields are NOT in protocol required_fields, making them convention-only. Commander-003 uses source_item_id instead of based_on, breaking cross-commander interop. Stable item_id guidance enables referential integrity but nothing validates it.
adoption_blockers: Verdict vocabulary unstandardized: accept_with_additions vs approve_with_suggestions vs endorse vs endorse_with_caveat
Linkage fields (target_item_id, target_commander, based_on) absent from required_fields - omission is protocol-compliant
evidence field has inconsistent types (string vs array) with no schema enforcement
No tooling validates events against protocol - append is fire-and-forget
Workflow sequence documented but not enforced - commanders can skip steps
Commander-003 uses source_item_id while others use based_on - no normalization
SKILL.md does not document the collaboration bus at all
findings.evidence_quality: Moderate-to-good. All 11 reviews contain substantive technical analysis with specific file references. All 7 improvements cite source proposals and include actionable recommendations with file:line evidence. Confidence scores calibrated (0.82-0.90). Gaps: no mechanism verifies source_refs point to real files, evidence field type varies making automated parsing unreliable, no deduplication check, timestamp precision varies across commanders.
